result.question: Research for a Baki the Grappler / Baki Hanma-inspired fitness+combat training campaign in a workout app (12-stage structure: user trains through a progression of Baki-universe fighters/arcs, e.g. YOUNG BAKI foundations → underground-arena striking → grappling/submission → the mentor & rival styles → a monstrous-strength benchmark → and a hardest final block culminating in a boss). Research question: What real-world training methods, fighting styles, and workout designs best map to Baki characters and their canonical training feats? Specifically: (1) Fan-made and fitness-creator 'Baki workout' / 'train like Baki Hanma' programs — what exercises, splits, rep schemes and structures do they use (the famous Baki shadow-boxing / imaginary-opponent 'phantom' training, high-rep calisthenics, bodyweight mastery)? (2) The canonical fighting styles and physical attributes of the key characters to ground per-stage mapping: Baki Hanma (all-around striker-grappler, imaginary shadow fighting, absurd core/abs), Yujiro Hanma 'the Ogre' (raw monstrous strength benchmark, boxing power, demon-back), Doppo Orochi / Katsumi (karate — makiwara conditioning, one-punch power), Retsu Kaioh (Chinese kung fu / baji), Jack Hanma (extreme size/steroid-fueled — DO NOT reproduce PED content, treat as a size/power theme only), Musashi/Pickle (primal strength), Kaoru Hanayama (grip strength / grappling with no thumbs), Izou Motobe (jiu-jitsu/aiki weapon defense). (3) Real training modalities that fit each: karate makiwara and knuckle conditioning (and safer alternatives), kung fu forms and stance work, boxing power development, grappling/wrestling and no-gi submission drills, grip-strength training (Hanayama), imaginary-opponent shadow fighting as a mental/visualization drill, and high-volume calisthenics/core work. (4) Safety guidance: knuckle/bone 'conditioning' myths and injury risk, overtraining, and why PED/extreme-bulk arcs must be presented as fantasy only. Output should give per-character exercise lists and fight-drill vocabularies usable directly as workout-module content, and flag anything that is anime fantasy vs. real trainable method.
result.summary: For a Baki-inspired 12-stage training campaign, the strongest real-world mapping comes from fan/fitness-creator "train like Baki" programs (notably SuperheroJacked), which structure training as a calisthenics/bodyweight-mastery core (Upper/Lower/Full-Body splits emphasizing high-rep, high-speed pull-ups and push-ups) plus 3 HIIT days, dedicated ab/core work, and layered MMA/boxing skill work built on progressive overload — with shadow-boxing and jab technique serving as the real-world stand-in for Baki's "phantom"/imaginary-opponent training. That creator maintains a per-character library (e.g. Retsu Kaioh Chinese-kenpo and Katsumi Orochi karate routines), which directly supports the per-stage, per-fighter mapping the campaign wants. On the canon side, characters cleanly split into trainable themes (Baki = all-around striker-grappler + shadow-fighting + extreme core; Yujiro = raw strength/power benchmark at ~6'3"/265 lbs favoring simple fundamentals and mental dominance) versus explicit anime-fantasy that must be flagged as non-trainable (Jack's PED "super steroids," 30-hours-a-day training, titanium-implant biting; Yujiro's genetic bloodline Demon Back; superhuman feats like bare-handed polar-bear kills). This lets each stage pair a real modality (calisthenics, boxing power, karate/kung fu conditioning, grappling, grip work, visualization) with clear "fantasy vs. real" labeling for safety.

findings.claim: Fan/fitness-creator 'Baki' programs are structured as a calisthenics core (Upper/Lower/Full-Body splits) plus 3 HIIT days, dedicated ab/core sessions, and added MMA work, built on progressive overload with high-rep/high-speed bodyweight mastery and layered weight training.
findings.evidence: SuperheroJacked's Baki program specifies 3 days Upper/Lower/Full-Body strength split, 3 HIIT days of varied types, three separate ab/core sessions, plus MMA training (sample: Mon Upper, Tue HIIT A+Core A, Wed Full Body, Thu HIIT B+Core B, Fri Lower, Sat HIIT C+Core C, Sun rest). It is calisthenics-primary ('shred through pull ups and push ups'), layers weight training justified by a 300+ lb bench feat, and states its goal as 'building strength through progressive overload, as Baki's calisthenics training level is extreme.' Directly usable as the campaign's foundational split structure. Caveat: fan-made, not canonical training.

findings.claim: Baki's 'phantom'/imaginary-opponent training maps directly onto shadow-boxing, which these programs integrate alongside jab technique and explosiveness work.
findings.evidence: Boxing-focused Baki routines 'include calisthenics training with shadow-boxing and jab techniques combined with exercises that work on explosiveness.' Shadow-boxing IS imaginary-opponent striking, so it is the real trainable component behind Baki's phantom-fighting theme; corroborated by other fitness sites pairing shadowboxing with explosive work (box jumps, explosive push-ups, sprints). Shadow-boxing/visualization also grounds a mental-training module across stages.

findings.claim: SuperheroJacked maintains a per-character Baki-universe library (Retsu Kaioh Chinese-kenpo, Katsumi Orochi karate, Kaiou Kaku, etc.), validating a per-stage per-fighter mapping approach.
findings.evidence: Distinct dedicated pages resolve for Katsumi Orochi (karate), Kaiou Retsu (Chinese kenpo/kung fu), and Kaiou Kaku, plus a 'Baki Inspired Workouts' archive category. This confirms an existing model for building separate character modules per stage, matching the campaign's structure. Caveat: the primary page returned HTTP 403 to direct fetch; confirmed via the site's own indexed URLs and search.

findings.claim: Yujiro Hanma is the series' raw-strength/size benchmark (~6'3", ~265 lbs, 'Strongest Creature on Earth'/'the Ogre'), whose canonical style favors simple fundamental martial arts and direct strength exchanges over borrowed complex technique.
findings.evidence: Fandom: 'about 6'3" and weighing about 265 pounds'; 'usually resorts to simple martial arts and exchanges of strength' and 'rarely uses the techniques of others.' This grounds a monstrous-strength/power stage (heavy compound lifts, raw power benchmarks) rather than a technical-skill stage. The style-preference claim was 2-1 (one dissent noting he has mastered many arts), but the framing 'favors raw strength over borrowed technique' accommodates both his capability and his in-fight preference.

findings.claim: Yujiro is written as deriving power partly from mental capacity, not muscle alone, grounding a visualization/mental-training component in his stage.
findings.evidence: Fandom: 'Strydum once told Baki that Yujiro's mental capacity is far stronger than any muscle on him, which is also a source of his power.' Justifies pairing the power/benchmark stage with a mental-focus/visualization module (aligning with the shadow-boxing visualization theme). Claim about fictional character writing, adequately sourced by franchise wiki.

findings.claim: Yujiro's 'Demon Back/Demon Face' is a genetic Hanma-bloodline trait, NOT a trainable technique — pure anime fantasy that must be flagged as non-trainable.
findings.evidence: Fandom: the Demon Face is 'not so much a technique as it is a predetermined advantage such as height or muscle-building capacity, since it is a gift dependent on genetics and bloodline.' Only three known bloodline users. Corroborated by FictionHorizon/CBR/Sportskeeda; the muscle SHAPE is anatomically discussable but the strength/speed multiplication is fantasy. Flag in-app as fantasy, not a training goal.

findings.claim: Jack Hanma is an explicit PED-driven fantasy character (canonically ingests 'super steroids' to survive otherwise-suicidal '30 hours a day' training) and must be presented as a size/power theme only, never a reproducible regimen.
findings.evidence: Fandom: Jack 'ingest[s] super steroids to perform training regimens that would instead be suicidal... training 30 hours a day would create such a body.' Corroborated by NamuWiki/Baidu (experimental drug 'x4,' scientist warned it could kill him). This directly justifies the safety guidance: the Jack/size-power stage must be themed (raw size/strength imagery) with explicit disclaimers, and PED content must not be reproduced.

findings.claim: Jack's fighting style is unrefined brute force (a brawler/pit-fighter relying on brutality over technique), distinguishing him from the roster's technical strikers/grapplers.
findings.evidence: Fandom: 'Jack's fighting style is unrefined due to his lack of technique.' CBR: 'more of a brawler, using a street or pit fighting style that relies on pure brutality over technique... punches, kicks, stomps.' Supports mapping Jack to a raw power/size stage rather than a technique module — contrast with Retsu (kung fu), Doppo (karate), Izou (jiu-jitsu).

findings.claim: Jack's signature 'Biting Path' with surgically implanted titanium-alloy teeth is an anime-fantasy body modification, not a real conditioning method.
findings.evidence: Fandom: 'He subsequently developed the "Biting Path" system and had titanium alloy teeth implanted.' Bite force likened to a crocodile. Corroborated by Baidu/NamuWiki. Flag as fantasy body-mod; do not translate into any real conditioning content.

findings.claim: Jack's canonical feats (bare-handed polar-bear kill, punching Sikorsky hard enough to lift the telephone box) are superhuman benchmarks positioning him as a fantasy power theme, not a realistic strength target.
findings.evidence: Fandom confirms both feats. These are physically impossible and correctly flagged as non-trainable — use as thematic 'monstrous power' imagery for the size/power stage with a fantasy disclaimer, not as a strength goal.

result.caveats: Sourcing skews toward fan/fitness-blog and franchise-wiki material rather than primary strength-science or peer-reviewed sources. All specific program-structure and character-fact claims trace to two domains: SuperheroJacked (a single fitness creator's fan program — descriptive of its own content, not evidence of efficacy) and baki.fandom.com (franchise wiki, adequate for fictional canon but non-authoritative). The SuperheroJacked primary page returned HTTP 403 to direct fetch throughout verification; wording was confirmed via mirrors (studylib/pdfcoffee) and search snippets, so exact rep-scheme numbers beyond the split structure should be re-verified against the live page. Character height/weight figures (6'3\"/265 lbs) are approximate anime/manga stats that vary by source. Crucially, the research surfaced strong confirmation of which elements are FANTASY (Jack's PEDs/titanium teeth/30-hr training, Yujiro's genetic Demon Back, all superhuman feats) but did NOT surface primary sourcing on several real-world modalities named in the research question — makiwara/knuckle conditioning injury risk, grip-strength protocols (Hanayama), kung fu stance work (Retsu/baji), and overtraining safety were not covered by any surviving claim, so those must be sourced separately before writing safety copy. Baki canon is not time-sensitive; the fitness content is evergreen but reflects one creator's 2020-2023 opinions.
openQuestions: What are the real, evidence-based safety facts on karate makiwara / knuckle 'conditioning' (bone remodeling myths, arthritis/injury risk) and what safer alternatives exist? No surviving claim addressed this despite it being central to the Doppo/Katsumi karate stage.

What specific grip-strength training protocols best theme the Hanayama stage (pinch/crush/support grip, thick-bar, farmer's carries), and what overtraining limits apply? Not covered by any verified claim.

What concrete exercises, forms, and stance-work map to the kung fu / baji stages (Retsu Kaioh) and the primal-strength stages (Musashi/Pickle) and jiu-jitsu/weapon-defense (Izou Motobe)? Only that dedicated SHJ pages exist — their actual contents were not extracted.

What rep schemes, progression rules, and volume caps do these fan programs prescribe in detail (beyond the weekly split), and are they supported by any real strength/hypertrophy evidence for the app to responsibly adopt?
